title: Fedora f42 podman start and toolbox enter isn't working for me at all, can't enter any container.
body:
### Issue Description

While this "Fedora f42" may sound like it should be reported against Fedora, it was not reproducible for @debarshiray and he investigated error logs but without luck and suggested it should be reported here.

All attempts trying to `toolbox enter` any container, e.g. here `fedora-toolbox-42`, fail with `Error: failed to start container fedora-toolbox-42`. Tried with several different images, all fail. Similar with `podman start`.

It seems noteworthy that the smoketest `podman run --rm registry.fedoraproject.org/fedora-toolbox:42 pwd` runs successfully.

`podman logs fedora-toolbox-42` does not say anything.

`podman start --attach fedora-toolbox-42` gives
```
Error: unable to start container d4cd1ee8c876a13e8cc17a6c0e37f30406e5eb8ed178c06bf140f9790e3b2109: crun: open `/home/username/.local/share/containers/storage/overlay/d15bd1c0ae5099f6109c0fbac389228194da84766651250082dba4b0dc19fdba/merged`: Permission denied: OCI permission denied
```
and that d15bd1c0ae5099f6109c0fbac389228194da84766651250082dba4b0dc19fdba directory is owned by the user's subuid:subgid (from /etc/subuid and /etc/subgid).

Podman version of podman-5.5.2-1.fc42.x86_64 is
```
Client:        Podman Engine
Version:       5.5.2
API Version:   5.5.2
Go Version:    go1.24.4
Git Commit:    e7d8226745ba07a64b7176a7f128e4ef53225a0e
Built:         Tue Jun 24 02:00:00 2025
Build Origin:  Fedora Project
OS/Arch:       linux/amd64
```

I would add a log file from `podman --log-level debug start --attach fedora-toolbox-42` but this pesky github just comes up with `<!-- Failed to upload "podman.txt" -->`, pasting instead.

```

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. on F42 with no containers nor images issue `toolbox create` command to obtain the current default `registry.fedoraproject.org/fedora-toolbox:42` image and create a container with name `fedora-toolbox-42`
2. issue `toolbox enter fedora-toolbox-42` command

That's it, `Error: failed to start container fedora-toolbox-42` is displayed.

### Describe the results you received

Just the error message.

### Describe the results you expected

Well, entering a working container?

### podman info output

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

None

### Upstream Latest Release

### Additional environment details

### Additional information

_No response_

comments.body:
I wish I knew how to get more insight into this without hacking up `podman(1)` or `crun(1)` by injecting more logs.  I don't know if `podman --log-level debug ...` is wired up to `crun --debug`.

> `podman start --attach fedora-toolbox-42` gives
> 
> ```
> Error: unable to start container d4cd1ee8c876a13e8cc17a6c0e37f30406e5eb8ed178c06bf140f9790e3b2109: crun: open `/home/username/.local/share/containers/storage/overlay/d15bd1c0ae5099f6109c0fbac389228194da84766651250082dba4b0dc19fdba/merged`: Permission denied: OCI permission denied
> ```

That `OCI permission denied`, that there's nothing in `podman logs`, and snippets like this in `podman.git`:
```
        // ErrOCIRuntimePermissionDenied indicates the OCI runtime attempted to invoke a command that returned        
        // a permission denied error                                                                                  
        ErrOCIRuntimePermissionDenied = errors.New("OCI permission denied")
```

... make me wonder if the entry point of the container is not starting at all.

That would be serious and would break the upstream Toolbx CI left and right, but that's not the case.

The entry point of Toolbx containers is the host's `toolbox(1)` binary itself, and it's got some brutal hacks to deal with ABI incompatibilities between the container and the host.  So, I won't be surprised if something is wrong, but in the past when we ran into ABI problems, the symptoms were clearer:
https://github.com/containers/toolbox/issues/821#issuecomment-871615837

What happens if you run `podman run --rm  --userns keep-id registry.fedoraproject.org/fedora-toolbox:42 pwd`?

From the error crun fails to open the rootfs podman pass to it.

That gives
```
Error: crun: open `/home/username/.local/share/containers/storage/overlay/4dd8f562bae6ea465dbf9a6d0bcc7fed7245cefbb22cde8fbec25413996be432/merged`: Permission denied: OCI permission denied
```

I expect `podman run --rm  --userns keep-id registry.fedoraproject.org/fedora-toolbox:42 pwd` to always work, otherwise it means capabilities are not honored by the kernel

What are the permissions on the home directory?

I think the issue is the file system used, might not understand user namespaces, can you show the content of `/proc/self/mountinfo`?

Oh dear.. $HOME permissions are `drwx--x---.`, if I change that to `drwx--xr-x.` or even only `drwx--x--x.` then everything works :-/  Is that expected? I mean, yes in custom userns it is, but having to open directory traversal for everyone doesn't look that appealing to me..

what filesystem are you using?  Please show the output of `cat /proc/self/mountinfo`.

Probably it doesn't honor CAP_DAC_OVERRIDE in a user namespace

`55 69 252:3 / /home rw,relatime shared:50 - ext4 /dev/mapper/isivg-home rw,seclabel`

(need to leave now, available on Monday again).
if you run `podman run --rm  --userns keep-id registry.fedoraproject.org/fedora-toolbox:42 grep . /proc/self/*id_map` with the tweaked permissions, what do you see?

thanks, can't spot anything wrong.

Who owns `/home/username`?  Can you show `ls -ldn /home/username`?
the gid ownership seems wrong, that might be the reason why it is not working
It's a group with directory traversal permission for some users, _including_ `username`.

yeah but it is not mapped inside the user namespace, and I believe that is the reason why you see the failure.

So you can either chown the directory to the UID:GID of your user, or leave it world accessible.

I'd prefer to not add yet another check into Podman because it will make it slightly slower for each user, to detect something that looks more like a misconfiguration
